summary: Lee is a second-year animation student at IDT with a heavy weekday schedule and project overtime, who relies on parents for household groceries and cooking but spends ~€30–€40 a week on college lunches. Lee uses Instagram and TikTok most (TikTok is the primary source of food content), and prefers ordering directly with local takeaways or their apps/websites rather than aggregators for dispute resolution. Takeaway is a Friday "unspoken ritual" and is usually spontaneous after long days or when wanting variety; social outings make takeaway feel less guilty and more social. A recent Just Eat order was physically taken by a neighbor at delivery, creating distrust around aggregator deliveries in apartment settings. Lee ranks always-on offers/deals highest, then price, then free delivery; subscriptions feel risky for students (fear of paying for something they won't fully use and then ordering more). Generally avoids grocery delivery (convenient local stores / family routines), but found delivery useful when distance made shopping impractical.
